project.title: Human triple negative breast cancer cell line MDA-MB-231 LC-MSMS
project.projectDescription: To detect the protein that interact with NONO and illustrate the important role and the biological mechanism it may involved of NONO in TNBC.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: MDA-MB-231 cells transduced with NONO-BioID2 fusion protein or BioID2-only control were seeded in 10-cm dishes. When cells were grown to 80% confluency, replaced with complete medium containing with 50 μM biotin (Sigma, B4501) and cultured for 16-18 h. Cells were lysed in lysis buffer (50 mM Tris Hcl pH 7.4, 500 mM NaCl, 0.2% SDS,  1 mM DTT, and 1× protease inhibitors), subsequently added 0.1 μl pierce universal nuclease (Thermo Fisher Scientific, 88701) to each sample and incubated for 10 min at room temperature. After sonicating and streptavidin affinity purification, the precipitates were washed with wash buffer (8 M urea in 50 mM Tris pH 7.4). Finally, biotinylated proteins were determined by quantification mass spectrometry.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: The mass spectrum database retrieval software used in this project is MaxQuant1.6.1.0. The Protein Database used is the Uniprot Protein Database and the species is Homo sapiens.

references.referenceLine: Li Q, Ci H, Zhao P, Yang D, Zou Y, Chen P, Wu D, Shangguan W, Li W, Meng X, Xing M, Chen Y, Zhang M, Chen B, Kong L, Zen K, Huang DCS, Jiang ZW, Zhao Q. NONO interacts with nuclear PKM2 and directs histone H3 phosphorylation to promote triple-negative breast cancer metastasis. J Exp Clin Cancer Res. 2025 44(1):90
